!Sample_growth_protocol_ch1: For single cell suspension kidneys were dissected and incubated for 40 min at 37Â°C in RPMI medium (including 0.01 M HEPES; 0.5 mL filtered FBS and 0.4Âµg/ mL DNase and 8 Âµg/ mL collagenase D). The samples were homogenized using a gentleMACS (Miltenyi Biotec).
!Sample_extract_protocol_ch1: Cells were loaded on a 10x Chromium single cell instrument.
All steps were performed according to standard protocol of the Chromium single cell 5â v2 kit to generate high-quality cDNA libraries.
!Sample_data_processing: 10x Genomics raw sequencing data were processed using CellRanger software (version 5.0.0, 10x Genomics, Pleasanton, CA) and the human genome GRCh38-2020-A release as the reference (function cellranger count). The matrices of cells and the unique molecular identifier (UMI) count were obtained and further processed by the R package Seurat (version 4.0.1)

To remove potential doublets, cutoff values for number of expressed genes (nGene) were determined for samples following visual inspection of the distribution of nGene and nUMI for each cell. The cells with higher nGene values were excluded. We further removed low-quality cells with high mitochondrial genes of all detected genes

!Sample_extract_protocol_ch1: Colon tissue was collected into MACS Tissue Storage Solution (Miltenyi 130-100-008) at 4Â°C. Tissue was then cut into 1-5mm fragments, before transferring up to 4 x 5 mg slices into vials containing 1 mL freezing media (50:40:10 FBS:RPMI:DMSO). To thaw, vials were warmed for minimum necessary time in 37Â°C bath, then immediately transferred to 50 mL conical tube filled with RPMI, and centrifuged at 400 xg for 5 minutes. Tissue was dissociated as described (REF: doi: 10.1016/j.cell.2020.06.001), briefly each pellet was resuspended in 5 mL of collagenase-containing enzymatic digestion solution prepared according to manufacturerâs instructions (Miltenyi Human Tumor Dissociation Kit #30-095-929), prewarmed to 37Â°C. Tissue was incubated at 37Â°C for 20 min with gentle vortexing at 2â3-minute intervals. Dissociated cells were transferred to 70 Î¼m nylon mesh filter, and filtered cells were centrifuged and resuspended in 3 mL ACK red blood cell lysis buffer (Gibco #A1049201) for 1 minute at RT, then immediately diluted with 10 mL of PBS with 1% FBS. Cells were centrifuged and subsequently resuspended in Live/dead Fixable Near-IR Dead Cell Stain Kit (Thermo # L34975) viability dye solution (1:1000 in PBS), followed by 15 min incubation at RT in the dark. Following viability dye staining, cells were washed and stained for sorting and single-cell RNA sequencing.

Samples were processed for sequencing using the 10X Genomics 5â Single Cell Immune Profiling workflow. Î³Î´ TCR libraries were generated using the 10X universal forward primers and nested custom reverse primers (TRG outer Reverse: AGTCTTCATGGTGTTCCCCTCC, TRG inner Reverse: AAAATAGTGGGCTTGGGGGA, TRD outer Reverse GTCGTGTTGAACTGAACATGTCAC, TRD inner Reverse CAGACAAGCGACATTTGTTCCA). Libraries for all samples were pooled and sequenced on a NovaSeq6000 or NovaX (Illumina) and resultant FASTQs were demultiplexed by the University of Chicago Genomics Facility.
!Sample_data_processing: Sequences were deconvoluted using Cellranger software v.3.0 and imported into R. Initial QC and gene expression and clustering analysis was conducted using the standard Seurat v5 pipeline.
Assembly: GRCh38

!Sample_extract_protocol_ch1: Thymic fragments were incubated in DMEM-F12 with rotation for 20min and thymocytes were collected from supernatant.Dead cells were separated using Ficoll density gradient and thymocytes were counted and frozen in FBS + 10% DMSO until thawing for staining and sequencing.

Samples were processed for sequencing using the 10X Genomics 5â Single Cell Immune Profiling workflow. Î³Î´ TCR libraries were generated using the 10X universal forward primers and nested custom reverse primers (TRG outer Reverse: AGTCTTCATGGTGTTCCCCTCC, TRG inner Reverse: AAAATAGTGGGCTTGGGGGA, TRD outer Reverse GTCGTGTTGAACTGAACATGTCAC, TRD inner Reverse CAGACAAGCGACATTTGTTCCA). Libraries for all samples were pooled and sequenced on a NovaSeq6000 or NovaX (Illumina) and resultant FASTQs were demultiplexed by the University of Chicago Genomics Facility.
!Sample_data_processing: Sequences were deconvoluted using Cellranger software v.7.1 and imported into R. Initial QC and gene expression and clustering analysis was conducted using the standard Seurat v5 pipeline.
Assembly: GRCh38

!Sample_treatment_protocol_ch1: Human liver tissue was minced and digested using Collagenase D (Roche, Grenzach-Whlyen, Germany) and liver cells isolated using a FACS Aria III cell sorter. Sorted cell populations were then mixed at equal ratios and cell suspensions then loaded on 10x chromium controller.
!Sample_growth_protocol_ch1: human far distant liver tissue from oncologic resections was used for scRNA-seq
!Sample_extract_protocol_ch1: Pseudonormal and far distant human liver tissue was used for cell isolation from 6 patients (4 female, 2 male, age range 45-76 yrs, receiving a lobectomy or hemihepatectomy for oncologic resection) and the following cell populations were FACS enriched at equal distribution: 1) alive, 2) CD45+ CD3E-, 3) CD45+ CD3E+ 4)CD45+ CD11C+ 5) CLEC4G+ 6) CLEC4G- CD31+, 7) EPCAM+.
Chromium Next GEM Single Cell 3' Reagent Kits v3.1 (Dual Index)
!Sample_data_processing: Data was mapped and processed using cellranger-6.0.0
Assembly: GRCh38-2020-A

!Sample_characteristics_ch1: cell type: Peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs)
!Sample_extract_protocol_ch1: CD19+IgM+IgDlow/- cells and CD19+IgM+IgDhigh cells from three deidentified human donors were purified by flow cytometry. Purified cells were then stained with Antibody Derived Tag directed against CD27, CD21, CD24 and CD38 and barcoded with a unique Oligonucleotide Hash Tag. After Hash- and Cite-tagged cells were counted and checked for viability using a hematocytometer, 40e4 cells (10e3 cells for each sample: 3 IgDlow, 1 IgDhigh) were loaded into a Rhapsody single-lane cartridge (BD Biosciences).

Library was prepared using BD Rhapsody System protocol rev. 2022-11 (BD Biosciences). Cellular mRNA and antibody-derived oligos were reverse-transcribed and indexed with a shared cellular barcode. Indexed cDNAs were then pooled and amplified by PCR according to the BD Rhapsody System protocol rev 2022-11 (BD Biosciences) and with specific primers amplifying the antibody-derived tags. AMPure XP bead size selection was performed in order to separate both the mRNA-derived cDNA (>300bp) and the antibody-derived tagged cDNAs (180-200bp). For the mRNA derived cDNA library and antibody-derived tagged library preparation, we further proceeded according to the manufacturerâs instructions (BD Rhapsody mRNA Whole Transcriptome Analysis (WTA), BD AbSeq, and Sample Tag Library Preparation Protocol, rev 2022-11). The Nextera N712 i7 primer (Illumina) and Truseq D501 i5 primer (Illumina) were used to for both CITE- and HASH- seq library amplification.
!Sample_description: 3' mRNA library: read1 file contains cell barcode and UMI; read2 file contains transcript
!Sample_data_processing: Pre-processing of sequencing results to generate count matrices (gene expression, ADT and HTO barcode counts) was performed using the BD Rhapsody pipeline run on Seven Bridges where ADT and HTO sequences were concatenated and aligned to the GENCODE GRCh38 genome build

Further processing was done with Seurat v4.3 (cell and gene filtering, hashtag identification, clustering, differential gene expression analysis) and Python (Bokeh data visualization, Palantir (v1.3.0) trajectory analysis)

!Sample_extract_protocol_ch1: The isolated cells from each sample were used for GEM generation, barcoding, cleanup, cDNA amplification according to the protocol provided from 10x Genomics Chromium Next GEM Single Cell 3' Reagent kit v3.1 with feature barcode technology.

Illumina library construction was done using the protocol and reagents from the 10x Genomics Chromium Next GEM Single Cell 3' Reagent kit v3.1 with feature barcode technology. Long-read libraries were generated using a modified FLT-seq protocol (Tian et al, 2021, Genome Biology).
!Sample_data_processing: Illumina data was processed using cellranger count (Cellranger Pipeline 5.0.1 with the option include-introns for sample Donor B sorted basophils and Cellranger Pipeline 7.0.1 for the rest of the samples with default parameters)

!Sample_extract_protocol_ch1: Peripheral blood samples were collected in Cell Preparation Tubes (BD Biosciences) and mononuclear cells were isolated by density gradient centrifugation. PBMCs were cryopreserved in the freezing media (5-10 million cells per tube; CryoStor CS10, STEMCELL technologies) and stored in liquid nitrogen until assay.  PBMCs were thawed in a 37Â°C water bath and contaminating red blood cells were lysed with red blood cell lysis buffer (eBioscience). After being washed, cells were suspended in Stain Buffer (BD Biosciences). After cells from each patient were tagged with the Single-Cell Multiplexing Kit (BD Biosciences), cells from different samples were combined for hash-tagging. Samples were adjusted to contain up to 1 million cells and, after Fc blocking, incubated with Abseq Ab-Oligos according to the manufacturerâs protocol. Abseq Ab-Oligos master mix was created by mixing the reconstituted AbSeq Immune discovery panel containing 30 antibodies (BD Biosciences) with 26 individual Abseq antibodies. After washing, single cells were loaded on the Rhapsody Cartridge (30,000 cells per cartridge). Cells were lysed and mRNA, Ab-Oligos, and Sample Tags were hybridized with the barcoded oligos on the cell beads.

Libraries of mRNA, Sample Tag, Abseq, and VDJ regions were prepared according to BDâs library preparation protocol (23-22204-00). Cell capture beads underwent template switching, Klenow extension, and exonuclease I treatment. Two-step nested amplification was performed and followed by an index PCR. PCR1 products were separated by double-sided AMPure bead purification into the shorter AbSeq and Sample Tag PCR products, and the longer targeted mRNA and VDJ PCR products. The AbSeq PCR1 product proceeded with index PCR. Targeted mRNA, VDJ, and Sample Tag PCR products underwent PCR2 amplification followed by index PCR. Quality of the final libraries were quantified using the Qubit dsDNA HS Kit and Agilent TapeStation high sensitivity D1000 screentape. mRNA, AbSeq, and SMK libraries were pooled together and sequenced by NovaSeq 6000 - S4 sequencer with 75 bp x 75 bp paired read. VDJ libraries were sequenced with the SP sequencer with 75 bp x 225 bp paired read. 400pM of the library was loaded, and 20-30% PhiX was included. Sequencing read depth was calculated by 1000 reads/Ab/cell for Abseq, 10000 reads per cell for mRNA, 1000-1200 reads per cell for sample tag, and 3000 reads per cell for VDJ.
!Sample_data_processing: FASTQ files obtained from the sequencing were processed in the Seven Bridges Genomics pipeline (BD Biosciences).  R1 reads were annotated for cell labels and unique molecular identifiers (UMI). R2 reads were aligned to the reference panel sequences and annotated for mRNA, AbSeq and Sample tags. Reads with the same cell label, same UMI sequence, and same bioproduct were collapsed into a single raw molecule. After errors within the UMI were corrected and putative cells were identified, expression matrices of mRNA and antibody-derived tag (ADT) were obtained. Multiplets and undetermined cells were removed by the sample tag expression data. Low quality cells were removed by using the following thresholds: low numbers of genes per cell (<30), low numbers of UMIs per cell (<50), and the low complexity of RNA score (Log10 Genes per UMI < 0.60). Doublets were additionally removed using Doublet Finder (v2.0.3)
Assembly: 1900mRNAref_file_Immune_response_panel_and_100sup_genes.fasta | BDAbSeq_Ref_ImmuneDiscoveryPanel_and_additonal_antibodies.fasta

!Sample_extract_protocol_ch1: Preparation of single cell suspensions for CITE-seq and spectral flow cytometry was carried out as previously described (Greenwell-Wild et al., 2021). Briefly, tissue fragments were minced and digested using collagenase II (Worthington Biochemical Corporation) and DNase (Sigma) and dissociated with the gentleMACS Dissociator (Miltenyi). Cells were then passed through a 70mm filter (Falcon, Corning), washed, and counted (Cellometer Auto 2000 - Nexcelom).

Single cell preparations of between 5x104 and 5x105 single cells from periodontitis samples were stained individually with TotalSeqâ¢-A Human Universal Cocktail, V1.0 (Biolegend, CA) as per the manufacturerâs protocol (Suppl. Table 4). 1.7x104 stained cells were then loaded with the reverse transcription mix on the Chromium chip G and partitioned into single cells in Gel Beads-in-emulsion (GEMs) using the 10X Genomics Chromium Next GEM Single Cell 3â Kit v3.1 as per manufacturerâs instructions (10X Genomics, CA). cDNA amplification and ADT libraries were prepared as per instructions from Biolegend while gene expression libraries were made as per the 10X Genomics protocol. All amplification steps were performed in an Applied Biosystems Ins (ABI) Veriti 96-well thermal cycler. Quality and quantity of the libraries were assessed using TapeStation (Agilent, CA) and a Qubit fluorometer (ThermoFisher). Libraries were pooled at a concentration of 2nM and sequenced on an Illumina platform (NextSeq 2000, Illumina, San Diego, CA) using the following read lengths: Read 1: 28 base pairs, Index 1: 10 base pairs, Index 2: 10 base pairs, Read 2: 90 base pairs.
!Sample_data_processing: CITE-Seq files were demultiplexed using CellRanger mkfastq
Demultiplexed data was processed using the CellRanger multi pipeline
!Sample_contact_laboratory: Laboratory of Host Immunity and Microbiome
!Sample_contact_department: National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases

!Sample_extract_protocol_ch1: Leftover clinical anti-CD19 CAR T cells (Kymriah, Novartis) of three donors were rinsed from the infusion bag and tubes and peripheral blood mononuclear cell (PBMC) were isolated and then cryopreserved. Without rest, thawed CAR-T cells and Nalm6 cells were washed in medium (in RPMI 1640 + 10% FCS + 2 mM L-glutamine + 25 mM HEPES + 20 IU/mL IL-2). After manual counting, 2 mL CAR T cells of each donor (5x10^5 cells/mL) were either co-cultured with 0.5 mL Nalm6 (1x10^6 cells/mL) (=stimulated) or 0.5 mL of additional medium(=unstimulated), respectively, in 6-well plates at 37Â°C, 5% CO2 for 24 h. These stimulated and unstimulated pre-infusion CAR-T cells were subjected to flow-based cell sorting. After an initial washing step with PBS (300 xg for 6 min at 21Â°C), samples were incubated with ZombieAqua (1:100, BioLegend) for 10 min in the dark at room temperature. Samples were washed once with 1 mL PBA (PBS + 0.25% BSA + 0.02% NaN3) and human CD19 CAR Detection Reagent (1:50, Milentyi biotec, 130-129-550) were added, mixed and incubated for 10 min at room temperature in the dark. After washing twice with 2 mL PBA at room temperature, an antibody mastermix comprising anti-CD19-PE antibody (1:50, clone 4G7, BioLegend), anti-CD3-APC (1:33, UCHT1, BioLegend) and anti-Streptavidin-PE/TexasRed (1:200, ThermoFischer, SA1017) was added and incubated for 20 min at room temperature in the dark, followed by two final washing steps in sorting buffer (PBA + 1 mM EDTA). Single, live CD19- CD3+ lymphocytes (8333 cells per donor and condition) were sorted on a MoFlo XDP (Beckman Coulter) sorter. Immediately after sorting, cells were pooled into one 10X sample according to their stimulation condition and loaded to a Chromium Next GEM Chip K (10X Genomics) and Chromium Next GEM Single-Cell 5â² kits v.2 were used to generate GEX and VDJ libraries according to the manufacturerâs instructions (10X Genomics, 1000266, 1000196, 1000256, 1000252, 1000287, 3000431, 3000510). Libraries were sent to Novogene and sequenced on an Illumina NovaSeq platform with PE150 strategy.
Library construction was performed according to manufacturer's instructions (10x Genomics) for 5' GEX and VDJ, V2 chemistry dual index
!Sample_data_processing: The sequencing sample was processed via the cellranger (version 6.0.2, 10x Genomics) `multi`-command using the gene expression reference GRCh38 (version 2020A, 10x Genomics) with added CAR-Gene-Sequence and the VDJ reference vdj-GRCh38 (version 5.0.0, 10x Genomics).
Assembly: GRCh38 (version 2020A, 10x Genomics), vdj-GRCh38 (version 5.0.0, 10x Genomics), barcodes.csv
!Sample_contact_department: Mikrobiologisches Institut â Klinische Mikrobiologie, Immunologie und Hygiene

!Sample_extract_protocol_ch1: Cyropreserved post-infusion CAR-T cells were thawed and rested overnight at 2x10^6 cells/mL in cRPMI (RPMI 1640 Medium + 10% heat-inactivated FCS, 0.05 mM Î²-mercaptoethanol, 0.05 mg/mL gentamicin, 1.1915 g/L HEPES, 0.2 g/L L-glutamine, 100 U/mL Penicillin-Streptomycin). After manual counting, all cells (1.35x106 C14, 3.55x106 C16 and 3.1x106 C20 cells) were collected and washed twice in 200 ÂµL sterile-filtrated Wash buffer (PBS, pH 7.4, with 1% filtered FCS) and then resuspended in the 50 ÂµL (C14) or 100 ÂµL (C16, C20) sterile-filtrated Stain buffer (PBS, pH 7.4 containing 1% FSC, 0.1 g/L Herring sperm DNA). 4.4 ÂµL (for C14) or 8.8 ÂµL (for C16, C20) of dCODE reagent mastermix (2.0 Î¼L of CD19-dCODEÂ® Dextramer (Immudex, DS005), 2.0 Î¼L of control-dCODEÂ® Dextramer (Immudex, WI03233), and 0.4 Î¼L 100 Î¼M d-Biotin for C14. For C16 and C20 these volumes were doubled.) were added and incubated for 15 min in the dark at room temperature. Mastermixes comprising a unique DNA-barcoded hashtag antibody (0.5 ÂµL per 1x106 PBMCs of TotalSeq-C anti-human hashtag antibodies 1â3 (BioLegend: for C14: 394661, for C16: 394663, for C20: 394665)), a CITEseq-antibody mix of DNA-barcoded CITE-Seq TotalSeq C antibodies (0.25 Âµg per 5x10^6 PBMCs of anti-human CD4 (BioLegend, 300567), 0.25 Âµg per 5x106 PBMCs of anti-human CD8 (BioLegend, 344753), 0.078 Âµg per 5x106 PBMCs of anti-human CD45RA (BioLegend, 304163), 0.078 Âµg per 5x10^6 PBMCs of anti-human CD62L (BioLegend, 304851), 0.3125 Âµg per 5x10^6 PBMCs of anti-human CD95 (BioLegend, 305651), 0.277 Âµg per 5x10^6 PBMCs of anti-human CCR7 (BioLegend, 353251) in Stain buffer (PBS, pH 7.4, with 1% FCS and 0.1 g/L Herring sperm DNA) and a mix surface markers and viability dye (anti-CD56-FITC (1:200, Invitrogen, 11-0566-42), anti-CD19-FITC (1:200, BioLegend, 302205), anti-CD4-BV711 (1:400, BD Horizon, 568371), anti-CD3-PE/Cy7 (1:200, Beckman Coulter, 737657), anti-CD8a-APC (1:400, BioLegend, 301014), anti-CD14-APC/Cy7 (1:200, BD Pharmingen, 557831) and ZombieAqua (1:500, BioLegend, 423102) in Stain buffer) was centrifuged (14,000 xg, 10 min, room temperature) and then added to the respective cells suspension. After 20 min incubation in the dark on ice, samples were washed 4 times with Wash buffer (300 xg, 5 min, 4Â°C) and then resuspended in 500 ÂµL sterile-filtered FACS buffer (PBS, 0.5% BSA). Sort enrichment was performed for single, live, CD56-, CD19-, CD14-, CD3+ dCODE-Dextramer+ cells on a MoFlo Astrios EQ sorter (Beckman). In total, 5,670 C14 cells, 69,925 C16 cells and 11,560 C20 cells were sorted into pre-coated 1.5 mL tubes containing FACS-buffer. Immediately after sorting, cells were loaded onto a Chromium Next GEM Chip K (10X Genomics) and Chromium Next GEM Single-Cell 5â² kits v.2 to generate gene expression (GEX), T cell receptor (VDJ) and cell surface (CITEseq) libraries according to the manufacturerâs instructions (10X Genomics, 1000263, 1000256, 1000252, 1000286, 1000250, 1000215, 1000190). Libraries were sequenced at Novogene (Cambridge, UK) on an Illumina NovaSeq platform with PE150 strategy.
Library construction was performed according to manufacturer's instructions (10x Genomics) for 5' GEX, VDJ and CITEseq, V2 chemistry dual index
!Sample_data_processing: The sequencing sample was processed via the cellranger (version 6.0.2, 10x Genomics) `multi`-command using the gene expression reference GRCh38 (version 2020A, 10x Genomics) with added CAR-Gene-Sequence, the VDJ reference vdj-GRCh38 (version 5.0.0, 10x Genomics), and custom feature barcodes (barcodes.csv).
Assembly: GRCh38 (version 2020A, 10x Genomics), vdj-GRCh38 (version 5.0.0, 10x Genomics), barcodes.csv
!Sample_contact_department: Mikrobiologisches Institut â Klinische Mikrobiologie, Immunologie und Hygiene

!Sample_growth_protocol_ch1: Memory CD4+ T-cells (mCD4) were isolated from the PBMCs  of people with HIV (PWH) by negative selection using EasySepâ¢ Human Memory CD4+ T Cell Enrichment Kit (STEMCELL Technologies), plated (10 cells/well, 96-well plates), and stimulated with irradiated allogeneic HIV-negative PBMCs (1x106/mL) in R10-100 with ARVs (RPMI 1640 supplemented with 10% FBS and 1% penicillin/streptomycin, 1% HEPES buffer, 1% L-Glutamine supplemented with 100 Units/mL of IL-2, 1 ÂµM Nevirapine, 1 ÂµM Emtricitabine, 1 ÂµM Tenofovir, 10 ÂµM Enfuvirtide, and 10 Âµg/mL 10-1074 bNAb) with CD3 and CD28 antibodies for 21 days. Feeder PBMCs were depleted of CD8+ T-cells and NK cells using EasySepâ¢ Human CD8 Positive Selection Kit II and EasySepâ¢ Human CD56 Positive Selection Kit II (STEMCELL Technologies) before being co-cultured with mCD4 T-cells from PWH. At the end of stimulation cycle, p24 ELISA was performed on all plates. Cells from p24 ELISA-positive wells were plated in serial 3-fold dilution (96-well plates), stimulated at the same condition as aforementioned, and subjected to p24 ELISA after 21 days. After the second round of plating and ELISA, cells from p24 ELISA-positive wells were expanded under the same stimulation condition. Clonal purity of isolated infected populations was determined using IPDA and further serial dilution subs were performed as needed.
!Sample_extract_protocol_ch1: Following 2 weeks of rest from stimulation with  CD3 and CD28 antibodies and feeders in R10-100 with ARVs, cells were stained with DNA oligo-barcoded antibodies and loaded onto the 10x Chromium single-cell workflow (10X Genomics) as previously described61 with a few modifications. Approximately 0.05-0.3 x 106 cells per sample were resuspended in R10 media and incubated with the TotalSeqâ¢-C Human Universal Cocktail (Biolegend) for 30 minutes at 4Â°C. In some cases, TotalSeqâ¢-C hashing antibodies #1, #3 and #4 (Biolegend) were spiked into individual samples, which allowed for multiplexing of ARCs and uninfected clone combinations into the same 10X lane. After staining, cells were washed 3 times in R10 media, followed by centrifugation (400 Ã g for 5 minutes at 4Â°C) and supernatant aspiration. After the final wash, cells were resuspended in PBS and filtered through 70-Âµm cell strainers

All experiments were performed using the 10x genomics V(D)J solution which incorporates scRNA-seq with additional profiling of protein surface markers . Antibody oligo counts are listed in the ADT and HTO tables. Stained cells from each sample were pooled and loaded into the 10X Chromium Single Cell Immune Profiling workflow according to the manufacturerâs instructions (CG000330, revision G)
!Sample_description: GEX (5' mRNA) library: read1 file contains cell barcode and UMI; read2 file contains transcript
!Sample_data_processing: FASTQ files from the 10x gene expression, surface protein, and TCR Î±/Î² libraries were processed using Cell Ranger v7.0.1 or v9.0.1 (10x Genomics), using either the count or the multi pipeline (when TCR libraries were available) with default parameters.

RNA transcripts from ECCITE-seq were mapped to a modified version of Cell Rangerâs GRCh38-2020-A reference package (GRCh38 / GENCODE v32 annotations), with the addition of the HIV-1 HXB2 reference sequence. TCR Î±/Î² libraries were mapped to Cell Rangerâs cellranger-vdj-GRCh38-alts-ensembl-7.1.0 reference package.

!Sample_treatment_protocol_ch1: For AIM data, PBMC were incubated for 24hours with overlapping peptide pools covering the entire SARS-CoV-2 spike protein. For CTV data, PBMC were incubated for 7days with overlapping peptide pools covering the entire SARS-CoV-2 spike protein.
!Sample_extract_protocol_ch1: AIM data: PBMCs from each vial were split in R10 across up to 8 wells of a U-bottom 96-well plate at 1e6 PBMCs per well. Pools of overlapping peptides (15mers with 11 amino acid overlap) which covered the entire SARS-CoV-2 spike S1 and S2 protein reons were added to each well at a final concentration of 1 Î¼g/ml and plates were incubated for 24 hours at 37 Â°C. One well of unstimulated cells per donor was included for use as a gating control for flow cytometric cell sorting. After incubation, plates were centrifuged at 1800 rpm for 2 min, washed in FACS buffer (PBS + 0.05% bovine serum albumin + 1 mM EDTA), and centrifuged again at 1800 rpm for 2 min prior to staining with the following antibodies: FITC-CD19 (HIB19), FITC-CD14 (M5E2), PE-4-1BB (CD137; 4B4-1), PE-OX-40 (CD134; Ber-ACT35), AF700-CD8Î± (SK1), BV421-CD69 (FN50), BV650-CD4 (OKT4), BV785-CD3 (OKT3), TotalSeq-C0032-CD154 (CD40L; 24-31), TotalSeq-C0155-CD107a (H4A3), TotalSeq-C0088-PD-1 (EH12.2H7), TotalSeq-C0171-ICOS (C398.4A), TotalSeq-C0063-CD45RA (HI100), TotalSeq-C0148-CCR7 (G043H7), TotalSeq-C0080-CD8 (RPA-T8), TotalSeq-C0072-CD4 (RPA-T4) and one of TotalSeq-C anti-human Hashtag antibodies (1-10; LNH-94, 2M2) per donor and timepoint. All antibodies were from BioLegend. TotalSeq antibodies were prepared as per manufacturer instructions and staining was performed in a final volume of 50 Î¼l for 30 min at 4 Â°C. After staining, cells were washed three times with FACS buffer, resuspended in 100 Î¼l of FACS buffer, and transferred to a 1.5 ml RNase-free Microfuge tube. SYTOX Green was pre-diluted 1:60 in PBS + 0.04% BSA, then diluted 1:100 in each sample. Samples were stored at 4 Â°C until sorting. Sorting was performed on a BD FACSAria III (BD Biosciences) using an 85-micron nozzle. All AIM+ T cells (CD69+ and OX40+ and/or 4-1BB+ CD3+) from all individual samples were sorted into a single collection tube (RPMI-1640 + 1% NEAA + 1% Na Pyruvate + 2.5% HEPES + 10% FBS), washed twice in collection media and resuspended at 13,700 cells per 38.7 Î¼l of resuspension buffer in preparation for the 10x Genomics Chromium Next GEM Single Cell 5â v2 (Dual Index) workflow.  CTV data: One vial of cryopreserved PBMCs per vaccinee at the pre-vaccination (baseline; âT0â) timepoint was thawed and washed in R10 (RPMI-1640 + 10% FBS + 1% Penicillin/Streptomycin) + Benzonase (500 U) on the day of use. 5.25 Ã 106 cells per donor were collected and labeled with CTV, as described (35). Briefly, cells were pelleted and washed twice with sterile PBS. After washing, cells were resuspended in 1 ml of PBS and 0.5 Î¼l of CTV was added (1:2000 dilution). Cells were stained for 10 minutes at room temperature and the reaction was quenched using 4 ml of ice-cold FBS. Media was removed and cells were resuspended in 5 ml of RhuAB10 media (RPMI-1640 + 10% human AB serum + 1% Penicillin/Streptomycin). Cells were allowed to rest for 5 min to allow excess CTV to leach away, counted, and resuspended in fresh RhuAB10 media at 2.5e6 cells per ml. Cells (2.5e5) were added to wells of a 96-well U-bottom plate. Unstimulated control (containing DMSO) and positive control (PHA at 2 Î¼g/ml) wells were included. The remainder of the cells were added in sequential wells so that all cells were used and stimulated with S1 + S2 peptide pools (each at 1 Î¼g/ml) in a final volume of 200 Î¼l. Cells were placed in a 37 Â°C, 5% CO2 incubator for 7 days. On day 4, the plate was centrifuged (1800 rpm for 3 min) and 100 Î¼l of media was removed and replaced with fresh, pre-warmed RhuAB10 media. On day 7, the plate was centrifuged (1800 rpm for 2 min), and all media was removed. Cells were washed in FACS buffer and wells were merged (3 into 1) to reduce the number of wells to stain. Staining was performed for 30 min at 4 Â°C using a cocktail of: FITC-CD19 (HIB19), FITC-CD14 (M5E2), AF700-CD8Î± (SK1), BV650-CD4 (OKT4), BV785-CD3 (OKT3), and one of TotalSeq-C anti-human Hashtag antibodies (1-10; LNH-94, 2M2) per donor. TotalSeq-C antibodies were prepared as described above. After staining, cells were washed three times with FACS buffer, resuspended in 100 Î¼l of FACS buffer, and transferred to a 1.5 ml RNase-free Microfuge tube. SYTOX Green was pre-diluted 1:60 in PBS + 0.04% BSA, then diluted 1:100 in each sample. Samples were stored at 4 Â°C until sorting. Sorting was performed as per AIM+, but CTVlo CD3+ T cells were sorted out.
The 10X Genomics Chromium Next GEM Single Cell 5' v2 library preparation was performed as per manufacturer instructions for Gene Expression, Feature Barcode and TCR library preparations.
!Sample_description: 5' mRNA library: read1 file contains cell barcode and UMI; read2 file contains transcript
!Sample_data_processing: FASTQ files were generated from BCL files using Illumina bcl2fastq. FASTQ files for all modalities were mapped to the GRCh38-2020-A reference genome and a custom ADT marker list using the Cell Ranger 7.0.0 multi pipeline for count, ADT and VDJ data. Cell ranger multi TCR readout is provided for each experiment/channel. Data analysis was primarily performed using the Seurat package (v4.3.0) (58) in R (v4.3.0). Quality control was performed as follows: low quality cells were removed (based on low UMI and gene count and high percent mitochondrial reads), doublets were flagged and removed using scDblFinder (59). Immunoglobulin and TCR gene segments, identified from the IMGT database (imgt.org), were removed from the gene expression object, except for the following genes: IGHM, IGHG1, IGHG2, IGHG3, IGHG4, IGHD, IGHE, IGHA1, TRAV1-2, TRAV24, TRDV1, TRDV2, TRDV3. Hashtags for each unique individual within a single experiment were demultiplexed using MULTIseqDemux (60) and then data from each experiment were merged into a single Seurat object. Gene counts were log-normalized (NormalizeData function), variable features were found (n=2000, FindVariableFeatures function) and features were scaled using default Seurat parameters (ScaleData function). The top variable features were used for nearest-neighbor and Louvain clustering (resolution 0.7) and to generate principal components, the top 30 of which were used to generate uniform manifold approximation and projections (UMAP). One individual was identified in the vaccine cohort that had potential previous asymptomatic COVID-19 infection. A sensitivity analysis was performed that indicated that this individual did not differ from other individuals in the vaccine group at either T1 or T2, as previously reported (61), therefore the potentially previously infected donor was retained for further analysis. Following Louvain clustering and expert manual annotation of identified cell clusters, several developmentally distinct cell populations were found to co-cluster based on gene expression and were manually separated as follows: MAIT_2 were separated from the co-clustered VD2 and CD8_Tem populations based on co-expression of SLC4A10 and TRAV1-2; VD1_Eff were separated from the co-clustered CD8_IFNG population based on lack of expression of abTCR chains.  CTV data was similarly processed.
Assembly: GRCh38-2020-A
!Sample_contact_address: Nuffield Department of Medicine, Roosevelt Dr, Headington

!Sample_characteristics_ch1: cell type: CD34-positive hematopoietic stem and progenitor cell
!Sample_treatment_protocol_ch1: Cells were maintained in standard culture conditions with StemSpan SFEM media supplemented with CC100 and thrombopoietin (TPO). No additional treatments or stimulations were applied. Cells were sampled at five time points: day 2 day 3 day 4 day 7 and day 10 of culture.
!Sample_growth_protocol_ch1: Mobilized peripheral CD34+ hematopoietic stem and progenitor cells (HSPCs) were obtained from four healthy human donors. Cells were cultured in StemSpan SFEM media supplemented with CC100 cytokine cocktail and thrombopoietin (TPO) at 37Â¬âC with 5% CO2. Media was changed every 2-3 days during the 10-day culture period. No additional media supplements were added to the cell culture conditions.
!Sample_extract_protocol_ch1: Total RNA was extracted from single cells using the 10x Genomics protocol. For CITE-seq samples cell surface proteins were captured using TotalSeq-B Human Universal Cocktail V1.0 antibodies before cell lysis. For Multiome samples nuclei were isolated for ATAC-seq library preparation following the 10x Genomics protocol.

CITE-seq libraries were prepared using 10x Genomics Single Cell Gene Expression with Feature Barcoding technology version 3.1. Two libraries were generated per sample: gene expression (GEX) using standard 3' single cell RNA-seq and surface protein detection using antibody-derived tags (ADT) with TotalSeq-B antibodies. Multiome libraries were prepared using 10x Genomics Chromium Single Cell Multiome ATAC + Gene Expression technology generating paired GEX and ATAC libraries from the same cells.
!Sample_description: CITE-seq GEX  library from mobilized peripheral CD34+ HSPC donor 13176 at Day 2
!Sample_data_processing: Raw sequencing reads were processed using Cell Ranger for CITE-seq samples and Cell Ranger ARC  for Multiome samples to generate count matrices.
Quality control filtering was applied to remove low-quality cells and genes. Cells with high mitochondrial gene expression or low total gene counts were excluded.

Time course analysis of mobilized peripheral CD34+ hematopoietic stem and progenitor cells cultured in StemSpan SFEM media with CC100 and TPO.  Cell types include HSC, MasP, MkP, NeuP, MoP, EryP, and BP based on preliminary annotation.; multiome_donor_31800_day_10_processed.h5mu; multiome_atac_counts.h5ad; atac_peaks.bed; atac_fragments.tsv.gz; SRTP0002020_atac_fragments.tsv.gz

!Sample_extract_protocol_ch1: CD8+ T cells from 3 PWH, 1 from each control status (n = 1 NC, ART+ NC, and C) of the previous n = 8 cohort, were co-cultured with CD8+ T cells as described for that set of experiments.. These co-cultures were performed in wells of a 24-well plate as per instruction of the Single Cell and Flow Cytometry Core at UAB. After 18 hrs co-incubation, cells were washed and first stained with the Hashing antibodies 1-10 (C0251-C0260) from Biolegend (cat # of C0251 is 394661 for reference to set) per manufacturer instructions post initial titration of both hashtagging antibodies(data not shown). After washing off, cells were stained using the same panel as previously outlined in addition to the following CITE-seq antibodies with the exact same staining protocol (TotalSeq-C a-CD3, CD4, CD8, TCRÎ±Î², CD56, CD69, CD137, CD107a, PE, APC, FITC, CD45RA, CD45RO, and CCR7). Unactivated (CD69+/137+) CD56- CD8+ cells from the unstimulated (no KF11 loading for 41A3 lines or pulse for PBMCs) as well as both unactivated and activated CD8+ T cells from stimulated conditions were sorted using the BD Symphony S6 cell sorter into a single tube, multiplexed for all 10 samples for one of the 3 PWH per day.

Libraries of these cells were then generated on that same day for RNA EXPRESSION (gene expression), TCR, and CITE-seq before these separate hashtagged libraries were then all sequenced on the NovaSeq using an S1 flow cell to cover 1.6 billion reads across the estimated 22,172 cells sorted post manual quality control pre-library generation. Specifically, Cell suspension, 10x barcoded gel beads, and oil were loaded into 10x Chromiumâ¢ Single Cell Chip K (PN# 1000287). Using Chromium Next GEM Single Cell 5' v2 Kit (PN#1000263) kit to capture single cells in nanoliter-scale oil droplets by 10xChromiumâ¢ Controller and generate Gel Bead-In-EMulsions (GEMs). The 5â-biased gene expression, TCR and feature barcoding libraries were generated following the instruction. All libraries   were sequenced by Illumina Novaseq6000 machine, targeting read pairs 20,000 /cell for gene expression and 20,000/cell for TCR libraries and 5000 /cell for feature barcoding libraries , and the sequencing cycles consists of 26bp for read 1, 90bp for read 2 and 10bp for i7 and i5.
!Sample_data_processing: Initially, 3 sets of paired reads fasta files were obtained for the 3 types of data (RNA EXPRESSION, TCR, and CITE-seq/multiplex hashtag antibody feature barcodes (FB)) for each multiplexed donor for a total of 18 files and 112 GB of gzipped data. Initial de-multiplexing was performed on these 3 donor multiplexed data using the RNA EXPRESSION and hashtags of the FB fasta files adopting a modified version of the strategy described here so as to in the most unbiased manner use the 5â Biolegend hashtagging oligos with the 3â CMO Biolegend demultiplexing pipeline to assign cells to different samples within each donor:https://www.10xgenomics.com/resources/analysis-guides/demultiplexing-and-analyzing-5%E2%80%99-immune-profiling-libraries-pooled-with-hashtags. A hashing_demux_HMO-set.csv targets file as well as a demultiplex config file were used for this purpose. After initial QC of the number of cells and reads returned for every sample, it became apparent that the elite controller PBMC-KF11 stimulated condition was either not sorted or not amplified. Bam files generated in the demultiplexing process were then converted back to fastq within donor sample folders under condition folders and TCR and CITE-seq data was then extracted using the cell idâs assigned to each sample from demultiplexing using cellranger multi and a new config file for each sample within each donor directory. This resulted in 87 unique fasta files, 3 each RNA EXPRESSION, TCR, and FB for the 10 conditions per donor sample under donor/condition directory folder, then used for downstream Seurat analysis.

All demultiplexed multi-omic data was imported into a single Seurat file by linking the TCR clonotypes, TCR filtered contic annotations, matrix, barcodes, and features files to each sample in a separate Seurat file through use of a complex folder structure (Patient -> condition -> GEXFB or TCR -> processed data files) before merging into a master Seurat file which was used for downstream analysis.

samples.sample_title: Ci10004 CD8s cultured with various conditions - GEX and FB combined for multi demultiplexing pipeline [GEX]
samples.description: Library name: Ci10004_S60; Ci10004_S60; Feature barcoding library containing gene expression (polyA RNA), antibody-derived tags (ADT), and hashtag oligonucleotides (HTO): read1 contains cell barcode and UMI; read2 contains mRNA, ADT, and HTO sequences. Libraries were generated using 10x Genomics 5â² v2 chemistry and sequenced on the Illumina NovaSeq 6000. "polyA RNA" was selected for the "Molecule" column because the bulk of the data for read 2 encoded for GEX. This combined file represents the output for the cellranger "multi" multi-omics and demultiplexing pipeline from a multiplexed input containing 10 multiplexed conditions.  After demultiplexing, processed files were generated - descriptions provided in supplemental file.

!Sample_extract_protocol_ch1: The single-cell RNA-Seq of FACS-sorted cell suspensions was performed on Chromium instrument (10X genomics) following the user guide manual for 3â² v3.1. In brief, FACS-sorted cells were washed once with PBS containing 1% bovine serum albumin (BSA) and resuspended in PBS containing 1% BSA to a final concentration of 700â1,300 cells per Î¼l. The viability of cells was above 80%, as confirmed with 0.2% (w/v) Trypan Blue staining (Countess II). Cells were captured in droplets.

Following reverse transcription and cell barcoding in droplets, emulsions were broken and cDNA purified using Dynabeads MyOne SILANE followed by PCR amplification per manual instruction. Around 10,000 cells were targeted for each sample. Samples were sometimes multiplexed together on one lane of 10X Chromium (using Hash Tag Oligonucleotides - HTO)  and specific extracellular protein epitope characterized (using Antibody Derived Tag - ADT)
!Sample_data_processing: CellRanger count pipeline
Assembly: GRCh38

!Sample_characteristics_ch1: tissue: Liver tumour and non-cancerous  tissue
!Sample_extract_protocol_ch1: Tumour and healthy liver tissues were minced and digested in RPMI 1640 with 10% FCS and 0.5 mg/ml collagenase D at 37 Â°C for thirty minutes, passed through 70 Âµm strainers, and lymphocytes were enriched using a 44%:70% isotonic Percoll gradient. PBMCs, when collected, were isolated on Ficoll Paque PLUS. Cells were cryopreserved in CryoStor CS10 (liver samples) or 10% DMSO: 90% FCS. Live, CD3+ T cells were sorted via fluorescence activated cell sorting.
Libraries were made according to the 10x Genomics protocol (Chromium Single Cell 5' v2)
!Sample_data_processing: Cellranger 6.1.2 multi pipline.
Features matrixes load into Seurat 4.3.0.
!Sample_contact_department: The Department of Microbiology and Immunology,  The University of Melbourne
!Sample_contact_institute: The Peter Doherty Institute for Infection and Immunity

!Sample_extract_protocol_ch1: 200 million splenocytes were resuspended in PBS containing 2% FBS and 2 mM EDTA, followed by incubation with Human TruStain FcX for 10 minutes. Cells were then labeled with a biotinylated lineage antibody cocktail (anti-CD3 [Cat# 100243], anti-CD19 [Cat# 302204], and anti-CD335 [Cat# 331906], all from BioLegend) for 15 minutes at 4Ë C. Following magnetic separation using the EasySepâ¢ Release Human Biotin Positive Selection Kit (STEMCELL Technologies, Cat# 17663), the unlabeled fraction was collected and subjected to LIVE/DEAD Fixable Aqua Dead Cell Staining Dye (Invitrogen, Cat# L34966) for 10 minutes. The cells were then stained with PE -anti-Lin (CD3/CD19/NKp46), PE-anti-CD89, FITC-anti-CD11c, APC-anti-HLA-DR, and FITC- anti-CD66b, purified anti-CADM1 antibodies for 30 minutes at 4Ë C. The target population (Lin [CD3/CD19/CD56]â»CD89â»CD66bâ»CD88â»HLA-DRâº) was isolated using a MA900 Multi- Application Cell Sorter (SONY). Purified cells were blocked using antibody staining buffer, which contained human TruStain FcX (BioLegend), and then stained with FITC-anti-chicken, PE-anti- SIGLEC6, and APC-anti-AXL for 30 min at 4Â°C. After washing three times, cells were then incubated with Biolegend TotalSeq_B_Universal_Cocktail_v1 (Cat# 399904) and custom antibodies purchased from the Biolegend TOTALseq catalogue for 30 min at 4Â°C. Stained cells were washed three times and encapsulated for CITE-seq.
Single-cell suspensions were loaded onto the 10x Chromium platform, followed by 5â² gene expression library, immune repertoire library, and cell surface protein library.
!Sample_data_processing: Raw FASTQ files were processed with Cell Ranger (v7.1.0) using the cellranger count pipeline.  Gene expression reads were aligned to the GRCh38 human reference genome (10x Genomics,  refdata-gex-GRCh38-2024-A), with intronic regions included (--include-introns=true). Antibody-derived tag (ADT) counts were quantified simultaneously using a custom feature reference CSV  provided by the featureâbarcode matrix contained unique molecular identifier (UMI) counts for both genes and  ADTs per cell. Filtered feature-barcode matrices were imported into R (v4.3.0), and individual Seurat objects (v4.3.0) were generated for each sample. RNA and ADT counts were stored as separate assays. Quality control (QC) filtering removed cells with fewer than 500 or more than 6,000 detected genes, fewer than 2,000 total transcripts, or >10% mitochondrial gene content. Doublets were identified and removed using scDblFinder (v1.14.0, default parameters). Post-QC, gene expression data were log-normalized and clustered using the first 30 principal components at a resolution of 0.5 to identify major immune cell populations. Clusters corresponding to DCs were extracted for detailed subclustering. Subclusters were manually annotated based on the expression of canonical DC markers. To correct for background signal from ambient antibodies, ADT counts were re-normalized using the Denoised and Scaled by Background (dsb) method (v2.0.0), which models and subtracts technical noise using empty droplets and isotype controls. The resulting denoised âADTNormalizedâ assay was used for all downstream protein-level analyses.
Assembly: GRCh38

!Sample_treatment_protocol_ch1: Following overnight rest, cultures were split and treatment cultures were stimulated with PMA/ionomycin (16nM/1Î¼M) and IL-2 (100U/mL) for 6 hours
!Sample_growth_protocol_ch1: After isolation (colon-derived) or thawing (blood-derived), cells were rested overnight at >1M/mL in complete RPMI-1640 (Gibco) containing 10U/mL of IL-2, 10ÂµM ZVAD-FMK caspase inhibitors (Selleck Chem,), and 500nM Raltegravir (Selleck Chem).
!Sample_extract_protocol_ch1: PBMCs were isolated by Ficoll gradient centrifugation with ammonium-chloride-potassium buffer-based red-blood cell lysis and viably frozen in liquid nitrogen. Intra-epithelial lymphocytes (IEL) and Lamina propria-resident immune cells (LP cells) were isolated in the method of Trapecar et al. DOI:10.1089/aid.2017.0208 and pooled after isolation. Epithelial stripping was performed in an EDTA+DTT buffer with mechanical disruption on a gentleMACS dissociator, with IEL cells liberated to the supernatant. The remaining lamina propria fraction was digested in CLSPA+DNAse buffer and subjected to additional gentleMACS dissociation and mechanical disruption by syringe. LP cells were isolated by filtration of the supernatant through a 70 micron filter. LP and IEL cells were treated with DNAse, filtered through a 40 micron filter, and rinsed in complete RPMI culture media. Cells were then subjected to a dead cell removal protocol (Biolegend) to remove epithelial debris.

scRNAseq libraries were prepared according to manufacturer instructions (10x Genomics scRNAseq v3.1 3â sequencing, catalog 1000269, protocol ). Gel-bead and cell emulsions (GEMs) were generated using the Chromium controller, subjected to reverse transcription, and first-strand cDNA was extracted from GEMs. Purified cDNA was PCR pre-amplified (12-cycles, 1-minute extension) with primers binding the P7 region and the Template Switching Oligo, cleaned up by solid-phase reversible immobilization (SPRI) beads, analyzed by HS DNA 5000 capillary electrophoresis (Agilent), and concentration calculated by Qubit (ThermoFisher).  From this material, 25% was used for Illumina NGS library construction involving fragmentation, end-repair/A-tailing, adapter TA ligation, and SPRI double-sided size selection to remove adapters and unfragmented products.  Libraries were amplified with Illumina indexing/sequencing i5 and i7 primers (13 cycles), then cleaned with SPRI beads before being quantified by Qubit and the fragment size determined using a Tapestation (Agilent).  Libraries were then pooled at an equimolar concentration and sequencing was performed using NovaSeq or NovaseqX sequencing (Illumina). CITE seq was performed as above, but with the following additional steps. The TotalSeq-B Universal Human antibody panel (Biolegend, catalog 399904) of lyophilized DNA-tagged antibodies was resuspended and spun down according to manufacturerâs instructions.  Meanwhile, cells were treated with Human TruStain FcX (Biolegend, catalog 422301) to prevent antibody binding by human Fc receptors.  Next, cells from each donor were stained with Totalseq-B hash-tag oligonucleotide antibodies (Biolegend, catalog 155831, 155833, and 155835) then added to three pools comprising untreated PBMCs, untreated CD4 cells, and PMA/ionomycin stimulated CD4 T cells. Pooled samples were stained in parallel with the TotalSeq-B Universal Human antibody panel, washed, and prepared for scRNA seq library construction as above but with additional PCR amplification of cell surface feature libraries following manufacturer instructions (10x Genomics protocol #CG000206).
!Sample_data_processing: Alignment and cell-calling was performed on the UNC high performance computing cluster with cellranger v7.1.0 (10x Genomics) against a custom human-HIV reference genome. Alignment was performed for all files using cellranger count function with default parameters but --jobmode=slurm to run on the UNC high-performance computing cluster.

CITE-seq analysis was performed with cellranger v7.1.0 (10x Genomics) using the human-HIV reference genome and a feature reference .csv. The feature reference file: "TotalSeq_B_Human_Universal_Cocktail_V1_399904_Antibody_reference_UMI_counting_CellRanger_addHTO.csv"  was derived from the manufacturer supplied document with 6 TotalSeq B anti-human hashtag antibodies (TotalSeq B0251-B0256) added as the final 6 entries in the feature reference file. Gene expression (GEX) and CITE-seq surface (ADT) data were split in Seurat from the contents of the cellranger output directories "filtered_feature_barcode_matrix", renamed, and uploaded as separate files for each GEX and ADT library.

!Sample_extract_protocol_ch1: B cells were sorted by magnetic sorting using anti-CD19 beads from PBMC

The stained cells were sorted into tetramer+ cells and tetramer neg cells and cells from 4 individuals were pooled to be included in a single gem. Tetramer neg cells were titrated up to a total of 100,000 cells, and then mixed with tetramer+ cells. This cell mixture was resuspended in 2% bovine serum albumin (BSA; Bovogen) in PBS for CITE-seq antibody staining. The cells were incubated with Fc receptor blocker (Miltenyi Biotec) at 4Â°C, and then stained with prepared CITE-seq antibody mixture and incubated at 4Â°C for 30 minutes. Next, the cells were washed 3 times, and resuspended to 1000 cells/Âµl, in a volume of 40â100 Âµl for subsequent library preparation. Transcriptome, antibody-derived tag, and BCR libraries were separately generated using 10X Genomics kits. All samples were sequenced using an Illumina HiSeq 2500, with an average of over 20 million 100-bp paired-end reads.
!Sample_data_processing: Fastq files were processed using cellranger-6.1.2. References for GEX and BCR are GRCh38-3.0.0 and vdj_GRCh38_alts_ensembl-5.0.0, respectively
Assembly: GRCh38

!Sample_extract_protocol_ch1: For isolation of intestinal lymphocytes from biopsies, samples were mechanically homogenized using a 70 Âµm cell strainer, washed with PBS and cryopreserved in 90% FBS and 10% DMSO. On the day of the experiment, frozen cells were thawed in RPMI-1640, centrifuged at 400Ãg for 5 minutes, and washed once with PBS. Cells were stained with a viability stain (BD Horizon, Fixable Viability Stain 780), antibodies against CD3E (PE/Cynanine7, SK7, BioLegend) and TCR Î³Î´ (PE, REA591, Miltenyi Biotec). Viable TCRÎ³Î´+ T cells were sorted into 384-well plates using MoFlo Astrios EQ (Beckman Coulter). For blood samples, those collected in EDTA tubes and were diluted 1:1 with phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) and overlaid in a 2:1 ratio on Ficoll (Ficoll Paque PLUS, GE Healthcare Life Sciences). Samples were centrifuged at 800Ãg for 20 minutes at room temperature (RT) with no acceleration or braking. The buffy coat was collected, washed with PBS, and centrifuged at 500Ãg for 10 minutes (RT, maximum acceleration and braking). Cells were cryopreserved at -80Â°C in freeze in 90% FBS and 10% DMSO. On the day of the experiment, frozen PBMCs were thawed in RPMI-1640, centrifuged at 400Ãg for 5 minutes, and washed once. Cells were stained with a viability dye, anti-CD3E, anti-TCRÎ³Î´, anti-VÎ´1, and anti-VÎ´2 antibodies. Viable VÎ´2+ and VÎ´1+ Î³Î´ T cells were sorted into BSA-coated 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes using a MoFlo Astrios EQ sorter (Beckman Coulter).

cDNA synthesis and library preparation of sorted gut Î³Î´ T cells were conducted using the FLASH-Seq protocol as described previously (PMID: 35637419; PMCID: PMC9546769). Library concentration and size were assessed before normalization to 1.9 nM. Sequencing was performed on an Illumina NextSeq550 High Output flow cell (75-8-8-75) with a target of approximately 125,000 reads per cell. Single-cell RNA sequencing of blood-derived Vd1+ and Vd2+ T cells was performed using 10x Genomics with feature barcoding technology, allowing for multiplexing of cell samples from different donors to reduce costs and minimize technical variability. Hashtag oligonucleotides were obtained pre-conjugated in TotalSeq-C (5â² chemistry) formats from BioLegend. Cells were stained with barcoded antibodies during the staining step before FACS sorting. Antibodies were used at a concentration of 1 Âµg per million cells, as recommended by the manufacturer (BioLegend) for flow cytometry applications. After staining, cells were washed twice in PBS containing 2% BSA and 0.01% Tween 20, followed by centrifugation (400Ãg, 5 minutes, 4âÂ°C) and supernatant exchange. After the final wash, cells were resuspended in PBS, filtered through 40-Î¼m cell strainers, and prepared for sorting. Sorted Vd1+ and Vd2+ T cells were processed using the 10x Genomics single-cell 5â² V(D)J workflow according to the manufacturerâs instructions. Single-cell TCR libraries were generated using the following primers: First PCR: 1 Î¼M forward primer (5â²-GATCTACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGC-3â²) and 1 Î¼M of each reverse primer (5â²-ATCCCA GAATCGTGTTGCTC-3â², and 5â²- CCCACTGGGAGAGATGACAA-3â²). Second PCR: 1 Î¼M forward primer (5â²-GATCTACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGC -3â²) and 1 Î¼M of each reverse primer (5â²-GGGGAAACATCTGCATCAAG-3â² and 5â²-GACAAAAACGGATGGTTTGG-3â²). Libraries were pooled in appropriate quantities to achieve the desired sequencing depth, following 10x Genomics guidelines, and sequenced using NovaSeq 6000.
!Sample_data_processing: Sequencing reads from FLASH-Seq were pre-processed using BBDUK from the BBMAP suite (v39.06) for trimming and Trimmomatic PE (CROP:75 MINLEN:50, v0.39) for cropping. Cleaned reads were aligned to the Gencode hg38 reference genome (v46) using the STAR aligner (v2.7.11b) with parameters --limitSjdbInsertNsj 2000000 and --outFilterIntronMotifs RemoveNoncanonicalUnannotated. Primary alignments were retained using SAMtools (v1.13). Gene expression was quantified with featureCounts (v2.0.6) using the parameters -p -t exon -g gene_name --fracOverlap 0.25 and the Gencode primary assembly annotation (v46). For V(D)J and C (V(D)JC) repertoire analysis, raw sequencing reads were processed with MiXCR (v4.6.0) using its Smart-seq2 wrapper function and the built-in RepSeq.io reference library (v4.0)39. Sequencing reads from the 10x Genomics platform were processed using CellRangerâs (v8.0.0) multi pipeline to quantify gene expression and antibody-derived hashtag counts. Alignment was performed with the pre-built hg38 reference (v2020-A). TCR libraries from 10x Genomics sequencing were analyzed with MiXCRâs analyze 10x-sc-xcr-vdj wrapper function, leveraging the built-in reference library for annotation.
Assembly: PBMC (10x Genomics): GENCODE v32; colon (FLASHseq): GENCODE v46

!Sample_characteristics_ch1: cell type: peripheral blood mononuclear cells
!Sample_treatment_protocol_ch1: Patients received bi-weekly intravenous infusions of lecanemab (10 mg/kg). Blood was collected immediately prior to infusion.
!Sample_growth_protocol_ch1: PBMCs were isolated from whole blood using density gradient centrifugation with SepMate tubes. Cells were washed, counted, and cryopreserved in 90% FBS/10% DMSO in liquid nitrogen.
!Sample_extract_protocol_ch1: Cryopreserved PBMCs were thawed at 37C and resuspended in RPMI-1640 + 10% FBS for immediate processing.
10x Genomics Chromium Single Cell 5 prime v2 with TotalSeq-C CITE-seq (137 antibodies). Gene expression and ADT libraries from same cDNA. V(D)J libraries enriched using 10x Human T Cell V(D)J kit.
!Sample_description: PBMCs from female AD patient (age 75-79y) with APOE E3/E4, lecanemab treatment, ARIA status negative (control), infusion #17, gene expression library
!Sample_data_processing: Illumina NovaSeq 6000. GEX: 28bp R1, 90bp R2. ADT: 28bp R1, 90bp R2. VDJ: 150bp R1, 150bp R2.

Cell Ranger multi v9.0.1, GRCh38 reference. QC: <10% mitochondrial genes, doublet removal (scDblFinder v1.20.2), ambient RNA correction (decontX v1.4.1). Integration: Seurat v5.3.0 with SCTransform. Cell types: multimodal RNA+protein with weighted nearest neighbor analysis. VDJ: scRepertoire v2.3.4.

Sequencing libraries were prepared using the Library Construction Kit (10x Genomics, Pleasanton, CA, USA) and sequenced on an Illumina NextSeq 2000. Cells were stained using TotalSeq-C hashtag and CITE-Seq antibodies. Cell suspensions were then washed, counted, pooled, concentrated, and subjected to 5â² single-cell RNA sequencing using the Chromium Next GEM Single Cell 5â² Kit v2 and Chromium Next GEM Chip K Single Cell Kit, targeting a recovery of 10,000 cells.
Sequencing libraries were prepared using the 10X Genomics Library Construction Kit and sequenced on an Illumina NextSeq 2000.

!Sample_extract_protocol_ch1: PBMCs were isolated from heparinised blood by density centrifugation using Ficoll-Paque PLUS (Sigma-Aldrich). Tissue samples obtained from different surgical liver resections (HCC and paired non-tumoral background tissues from HCC resections; and histologically healthy liver tissue from liver metastasis resections) were collected in cold DMEM medium (Life Technologies) and processed within 2 hours. Tumour and tissue single cell suspensions were obtained by enzymatic digestion with the BD HorizonTM Dri Tumour & Tissue Dissociation Reagent (TTDR) (BD Biosciences) as per manufacturer instructions.

scRNAseq libraries (5Â´ gene expression library GEX, T cell receptor enriched library VDJ, and surface protein library containing the TotalSeq-C CITE-seq antibodies) were prepared using the Chromium Single Cell Immune Profiling kit (10x Genomics, v1.1) according to the manufacturerâs instructions. The generated scRNAseq libraries were sequenced using a total of 100 cycles (paired end reads) with a NovaSeq 6000 S2 flow cell (Illumina).
!Sample_data_processing: Sequence reads from all samples were processed and aggregated using Cell Ranger (10X Genomics, v7.2).
